inputs.normalization_note: Reported FY2025 FCF/sh; 2026 capex guide $180–190B compresses near-term FCF — Hohn bridge uses adj. EPS path for medium-term

ai_overlay.status: partial — narrative cites AI; math mostly FY2025 FCF + generic Cloud growth

in_model.cloud_backlog: >460B Q1 2026 CEO (8-K) — not a dated revenue schedule
in_model.capex_2026_guide: $180–190B noted; not subtracted from FCF0 in Lawrence base

not_in_model_requires_refresh: TPU / custom silicon as separate revenue and margin line
Inference cost curve (mgmt cost-per-query claims — verify transcript)
Cloud backlog to revenue conversion timing
Services AI monetization separate from 9% Services growth
Data-center JV economics (Blackstone JV — press until filing)

ai_inflection_bull.drivers: Cloud backlog, TPU external sales, Search AI yield, Cloud margin expansion

segments.label: Google Services (Search, YouTube, Play, Android)
segments.owner_cash_y0_source: FY2025 FCF allocated 90.9% by segment OI share [Assumption]
segments.notes: Core ad + subscription engine; FY2025 OI $139.4B

segments.label: Google Cloud
segments.owner_cash_y0_source: FY2025 FCF allocated 9.1% by segment OI share [Assumption]

options.label: Other Bets (Waymo, etc.)
options.notes: TCI Q2 2018: zero base; FY2025 op loss $7.5B

corporate_drag.notes: FY2025 Alphabet-level op loss $16.8B; FY2026 capex guide $180-190B

reconciliation.slack_notes: Segment sum @ 10% ~$164 vs $386 — gap = lower discount (~1%), higher Cloud growth, or options not in base

bear.notes: Search disruption + capex without Cloud ROI

base.notes: Cloud AI mix shift; normalize post-capex ramp

growth_explanation.theory_label: Cloud backlog conversion + Services Search yield (consolidated blend)
growth_explanation.problem_situation: Lawrence base IRR 2.1% at $386 on FY2025 FCF/sh — why 11% years 1–5 and not 6% bear or 14% bull?

mechanisms.chain: Cloud backlog (>$460B, Q1 2026 CEO) converts to revenue at high-teens to 40%+ YoY → Cloud OI share of consolidated cash rises → lifts blended FCF growth above Services-only path
Search +19% Q1 revenue with AI Overviews rollout → Services owner cash grows high single digits; consolidated 11% embeds ~9% Services segment weight
2026 capex guide $180–190B depresses near-term FCF; 11% consolidated assumes post-peak capex and Cloud ROIC lift owner cash per share after 2027

segment_theories.theory: Q1 2026 Cloud +63% rev YoY; backlog supports 25% segment owner-cash growth unless conversion slows
Conservative vs Q1 Search burst; AI monetization inside 9% not modeled separately

risky_predictions.text: Cloud revenue growth stays above 40% YoY through FY2026 if 25% segment path and 11% consolidated hold
risky_predictions.falsifier_action: Cut Cloud segment growth below 20%; revisit consolidated to 8% or lower

risky_predictions.text: Consolidated operating margin holds above 34% in 2026 despite capex step-up
risky_predictions.falsifier_action: Margin compression without Cloud mix offset → cut years 1–5 growth

falsifiers.observation: Cloud revenue growth below 30% for two consecutive quarters
falsifiers.action: Cut Cloud segment growth; reduce consolidated years 1–5 toward 8%

falsifiers.observation: Search revenue growth below 10% for two quarters while Cloud growth also decelerates
2026–2027 FCF per share flat or down vs FY2025 with no capex guide reduction
falsifiers.action: Falsifies capex-normalization mechanism; hold 11% only with explicit [HUMAN REVIEW]

ad_hoc_rescue_banned: Raising consolidated growth to 14% bull without backlog-to-revenue schedule in segment build
Using AI inflection $8/sh FCF as silent base-case FCF₀
Bumping exit multiple to save IRR while keeping growth unchanged

theory_implied.derivation: segment_build owner-cash weighted blend (growth only; exit from scenarios.base)

option_scan.question: Backlog / RPO / AI capex not in FCF path?

paths.label: Segment-derived growth (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

paths.label: Segment cash-flow build (reverse DCF)
paths.weight_why: Reverse DCF from segment filing data; cross-checks consolidated growth without replacing falsifier path.
weight_falsifiers: Segment sum PV tie-out fails vs consolidated OCF
Implied return moves >1pp when segment growth matches filing falsifier-adjusted exactly (double-count signal)
paths.hard_to_vary: partial: shares growth inputs with Tier A; weight kept below filing path

qualitative_adjustments.factor: No dated predictive attribute at spot
qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Scarcity is descriptive; no timed mispricing catalyst in base
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Cross-check names dated catalyst with timeline

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

themes.label: AI compute -> power demand -> grid/water constraint -> scarce Permian surface

indicators.label: TPL water segment revenue (USD m, FY)

insider_signal.disclaimer: Context only. Insider activity informs scenario confidence and stance discussion; it does not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

scenario_confidence.note: Scenario weights are qualitative confidence only; Lawrence base IRR unchanged.

top_dissents.label: Horizon Kinetics
top_dissents.falsifier: No dated contractual payoff or royalty/trust structure

top_dissents.label: Murray Stahl
top_dissents.falsifier: No asset intensity / pricing power (moat eroding + non-croupier)

lens_consensus.disclaimer: Advisory lens blend only. Does not replace Lawrence total-synthesis stance gate without [HUMAN REVIEW].

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_methodology.decision_rule: Use one complete non-overlapping component schedule. The legacy Lawrence return path remains a separate stance gate until the primary evidence bridges are complete.

component_valuation.coverage_statement: Phase 3 scaffold identifies every currently known material operating, financial, liability, reserve, and option claim once. Primary ownership and cash/NAV bridges remain open follow-up gates.

valuation.evidence: Filing-backed proof from GOOGL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260205_rpt20251231_acc0001652044_26_000018.htm; overlap_key=primary_operating_segment. 2026 capex guide $180-190B noted in GOOGL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260430_rpt20260331_acc0001652044_26_000048.htm.
valuation.cross_check: Component schedule sums to economic value; Lawrence consolidated IRR remains separate stance gate.
valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows segment economics, net cash, or option burden materially worse than low case.

source.locator: FY2025 weighted-average diluted shares ~12.45B; 12,447M used in owner FCF bridge
Segment note FY2025 Google Services operating income $139.4B

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of GOOGL, including every currently identified operating claim, financial claim, liability, reserve, and material option.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components or explicitly reserved. No component is intended to overlap another.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Existing research and filing-derived valuation inputs. The primary-source reconciliation remains an open evidence gate.

component_groups.label: Google Services owner cash
component_groups.valuation_basis: Phase 3 direct per-share scaffold. It is a provisional valuation range, not a committee-approved price target.
component_groups.adjustments: The low/base/high range incorporates ownership, tax, capital, cycle, timing, and realization uncertainty pending the primary bridge.
component_groups.overlap_control: Unique overlap key primary_operating_segment; no other component capitalizes the same claim.

items.label: Google Services owner cash
mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?
Is the security's ownership claim and economic-unit denominator reconciled correctly?
Does the evidence support the low/base/high range and its timing?
Are comparable assets truly like-for-like after production, contract, location, capital, ownership, and realization adjustments?
For an option, are success probability, time to realization, and remaining owner-funded capital explicit?
Is the component additive, or already embedded in another component?
What observation would move the base estimate materially?

valuation_method_route.label: High-return compounder
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism
valuation_method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism
method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

required_refresh_triggers: new filing or material operating update

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

universal_valuation_contract.decision_rule: No security is decision-grade until every material claim is valued exactly once by a valid calculation proof and every material evidence blocker is resolved.

capacity.note: Capex and power constrain training/inference growth.

pricing.note: Cloud pricing and AI product monetization still evolving.

latest.speaker: GA-Courtenay
latest.quote_note: Paper cites Musk 2026 / Smith decades as convergence signal

world_model_context.disclaimer: Context only. World Model fails and Superorg gaps flag [HUMAN REVIEW]; they do not auto-rewrite Lawrence base IRR. Promotion requires _system/reviews/pending/world_model_promote_{TICKER}_{date}.md.

pricing_analysis.economic_claim_note: Per-share claims use fully diluted shares from valuation.inputs.
pricing_analysis.implied_growth_contract: Constant 7-year owner-cash growth with a 25x terminal owner-cash multiple; diagnostic, not forecast.
pricing_analysis.strongest_counter_explanation: growth projected without its capital cost
falsifiers: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism
pricing_analysis.pricing_conclusion: Entry prices were computed mechanically from the routed power-zone profile (High-return compounder). They are decision inputs, not a decision; the owner must review the scenarios before acting.

trigger.policy: A complete economic-value analysis is required when any trigger fires; otherwise it is recommended.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of GOOGL, including every currently identified operating claim, financial claim, liability, reserve, and material option.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components or explicitly reserved. No component is intended to overlap another.

economic_value_bridge.accounting_reference: Existing research and filing-derived valuation inputs. The primary-source reconciliation remains an open evidence gate.
